aerogel refiner.how_to_use: The Aerogel Refiner is a Mythic-tier upgrader added in Part II of the 2019 Christmas Event. The Aerogel Refiner requires flaming ore in order to function. If the ore is on fire, then the multiplier starts at x2. The multiplier is reduced by x1/3 per upgrade to a minimum of x1, at which point it serves no purpose. 3 Aerogel Refiners are needed to reach the upgrade limit. This gives a total multiplier of ~4.44x. Do note that the Aerogel Refiner does not blink according to its usage limit, but instead when the ore is on fire. aether refinery.how_to_use: The Aether Refinery is a added in the 2016 September Update. It processes ores at x90 their value, however once an ore is processed the barrier changes from blue to red, blocking off any incoming ore for the duration of 5 seconds. After 5 seconds, the barrier will turn back to blue indicating that it can process ores again. If an ore has been upgraded by an Aether Schrodinger, at least once, the x90 process multiplier is bumped up to x300.

aether schrodinger.how_to_use: The Aether Schrodinger is a , a fusion between the Aether Refinery and the Flaming Schrodinger, added in The Ultimate Update. It has similar stats to the gambler upgraders as it has a randomized effect to each ore, but there's a twist from the Aether Refinery's side: it puts up a barrier at the back end to temporarily block incoming ore for 1 second. Since the barrier is activated at the back end of the item, grouping up ore together can allow multiple ores to be upgraded at once, contrary to what the description states. In the 100 Million Visits Event, a new effect was added to it involving the Aether Refinery. If an ore upgraded by the Aether Schrodinger is processed in it, then it's processed by x300 instead of by x90. A similar effect is given for the Aethereal Synthesizer, adding 500x for every Aether Schrodinger used, and capping at 5000x. List of Possible Effects EffectChancex4.245%Ore is destroyed25%x95%x20, ore is given sparklesx7, ore is flung + Ore value set to Explosion The Aether Schrodinger's average multiplier is x3.79 assuming that flung ores are recovered. Appearance When the Aether Schrodinger denies ores, blue flames will appear on its side showing resemblance to the Flaming Schrodinger. It also has a few parts and textures added to show the influence of the Aether Refinery.
aether schrodinger.name: Aether Schrodinger

ambrosia garden.how_to_use: The Ambrosia Garden is a , a fusion between the Ambrosia Fountain and the Sakura Garden, added in the Reincarnation Update. This was revealed by OutOfOrderFoxy on two tweets: one with a picture of it, and another hinting about its existence. It processes ore at a multiplier depending how long the ore has existed, ranging from x10k at less than 1 minute to x25k at >10 minutes. As for the Sakura Garden side of the furnace, ores that enter the furnace will permanently reduce its multiplier. Normal ores will reduce its multiplier by -10, while flaming ores will reduce its multiplier by -100. Flaming ores also cannot be processed by this furnace.

ancient coliseum.how_to_use: The Ancient Coliseum is an , the successor to the Ancient Temple. The function of this upgrader is simple. If the ore value is a decimal, it multiplies the ore by x10. If the ore value is a whole odd number, it multiplies the ore by x13, and if the ore value is a whole even number, it multiplies the ore by x19. Appearance Despite it being called a coliseum, it’s more of a complete Greek styled temple. More overgrowth is present and two white torches illuminate the item. The roof of the Ancient Temple has been made longer and the vines have grown. The color scheme has also been changed from black and white to just greyish-white. Old Functionality Before the December 6th, 2019 update, this and the Ancient Temple had a unique way of multiplying ore value. The Ancient Temple would divide the ore's value by 7 and use the remainder as its multiplier (x1-x6). For example, if an ore's value was 10, it would do 10/7 = 1 remainder 3. Thus, the multiplier was 3. However, the Lua code for the calculation had a limit, and it assumed any ore value over $1qd was divisible by 7, and thus, made its multiplier x1. The same problem arose with the Ancient Coliseum. It divided ore value by 7, but instead of it using x1 - x6, it used x4 - x24. The Lua had a limit, and all the ore values equal or over $1qd were multiplied by x1. However, both the Ancient Temple and Ancient Coliseum were reworked on December 6th, 2019. Category:Evolved Reborn Category:Upgrader Category:Reborn-Proof Category:Large Category:Winter Event 2017 Category:Superstitious Requirement Category:Length 5 Upgrader

angel s blessing.how_to_use: The Angel's Blessing is an that was introduced in the Gargantuan Update, and was one of the two first Enchanted items introduced, along with the Gargantium Mine. The base multiplier begins at a massive x150, but will lower based on the number of machine tags the ore has. It is advised that you use this upgrader either at the start of your setup or after a resetting device to ensure you get the full x150. It's multiplier is reduced by -5 for the first machine tag, by -1 for the next 14 machine tags, and then by -2 for the remaining machine tags until it reaches x1. It also has a few miscellaneous effects, including giving ores sparkles and having an upgrade counter of 0. The upgrade beam itself excels at range, being an exceptional 4 units long, compared to the typical 2 units.

It was foreshadowed in this tweet by OutOfOrderFoxy. The furnace has a multiplier which ranges from x400 to x25,600. However, these multipliers depend on two variables: 1. User's current cash. 2. If the user is on their base, or not.
anguished guardian of the gate.name: Anguished Guardian of the Gate

anti gravity scanner.description: An item that not only violates the standard upgrader, but also violates the standards of scanners. Rather than a horizontal scanner, this is a vertical scanner!

extra.drawbacks: Does not stack with other Scanners
extra.effects: Upgrades ores by x7

multipliers.raw_effects: Upgrades ores by x7

anti gravity scanner.how_to_use: The Anti-Gravity Scanner is a added in the New Heights Update. It upgrades ores with a multiplier of x7, provided they reach the beam.
anti gravity scanner.name: Anti-Gravity Scanner

apollium mine.description: This mine is rather similar to Pilotite, however it energizes the oxygen it creates, forming this plasma-like ore.

extra.drawbacks: Ore cannot be extinguished once on fire. Ore is not immune to fire.
extra.effects: 25x buff when ore is set on fire.

multipliers.raw_effects: 25x buff when ore is set on fire.

apollium mine.how_to_use: The Apollium Mine is an added in the Reincarnation Update. This is a rare dropper with a high ore value ranging in the trillions, as well as having a unique gimmick where its ore receive a large 25x upgrade upon being ignited. However, this flame cannot be extinguished, forcing the player to think twice about using upgraders that induce fire. A good way to use this special upgrade is to use your flame-inducing upgraders at the end of your setup.
apollium mine.name: Apollium Mine

arachnid mine.description: A powerful but very scary mine. Don't look into its eyes. Halloween 2017 Item.
arachnid mine.how_to_use: The Arachnid Mine is a Collectible-tier Multi-Spout Dropper added during the Halloween 2017 Event and was obtained though Pumpkin Boxes. It is named after the arachnid, a group of arthropods which include spiders and scorpions. The ores this mine drops do not line up with the dropper parts.
arachnid mine.name: Arachnid Mine

arcane lightning.description: One of the most powerful upgraders in the world. Rapidly click the button to grow an insane charge. The charge won't deplete unless it is neglected.

extra.drawbacks: Capped at x51 Multiplier decays after 1 minute of inactivity Requires manual clicking to charge
extra.effects: Base multiplier of x1 Each click increases multiplier by +1

multipliers.raw_effects: Base multiplier of x1 Each click increases multiplier by +1

arcane lightning.how_to_use: The Arcane Lightning is an Advanced Reborn-tier upgrader added in the Innovation Event: Sponsored by Black Panther. Similarly to the other lightning-themed items, this item is activated by a green button on the side. The upgrade starts out at x1 with every button click adding x1 to the total multiplier up to an upgrade of x51, but it can only upgrade once without a resetting device. The upgrade multiplier will quickly diminish after one minute of inactivity. The wire connecting two electrical towers will immediately light up upon one button click. The conveyor and the containers will slowly brighten up. The brighter the conveyor, the more charge the upgrader has. The conveyor speed will increase when the upgrader is charged.
arcane lightning.name: Arcane Lightning

aromatic holidays.description: Ah yes. The nostalgic setting of the holidays. Even in the freezing temperature, this item warms your heart.

extra.drawbacks: Does nothing to ores.
extra.effects: Present: +1 to Present Value Sets present on fire Makes present fireproof

multipliers.raw_effects: Present: +1 to Present Value Sets present on fire Makes present fireproof

aromatic holidays.how_to_use: The Aromatic Holidays is a added in the Winter Event 2020. During Christmas/Winter Events, it increases the value of presents by +1, allowing for a better present from Santa or Jak'uhfdor. It does nothing to ores, however. Category:Reborn-Proof Category:Upgrader Category:Christmas Category:Winter Event 2020 Category:Collectible Category:Sacrifice-Proof Category:Present Upgrader Category:Length 3 Upgrader Category:Limited Category:Fire
aromatic holidays.name: Aromatic Holidays

assembly unit.description: Fill the Assembly Unit with Hyper Ore to create a Hyper Ignition Fuse. Hyper Ore must go through an upgrader before being dumped.

extra.effects: A Hyper Ignition Fuse is created once filled up.

multipliers.raw_effects: A Hyper Ignition Fuse is created once filled up.

assembly unit.how_to_use: The Assembly Unit is a that only accepts upgraded ore from the Hyper Mine; anything else and the ore is simply destroyed. The user can receive money by clicking the orb once it becomes neon; this shows that the Assembly Unit is full. It takes 200 upgraded Hyper ore until it becomes full and creates a Hyper Ignition fuse, at which point the player can click the orb. Rewarding them a badge for creating the fuse, the money that the orb contained, and a new collectible to keep. When upgraded Hyper Ore goes into it, a white "liquid" can be seen slowly rising for each ore.
assembly unit.name: Assembly Unit

astral predictor.description: Sends a signal into time to upgrade ore some time in the future. Can only be used once.

extra.effects: Multiplies ore value by 1.08x every 0.2s for a total duration between 2.2s and 5.2s Average of 4.00x over 3.6s
extra.old_effects: Before Reincarnation Update Multiplied ore value by x4.5 to x6.5 in 5 seconds to 2 minutes.
extra.submission_description: These mysteryious islands above the clouds upgrader ore by 5x!

multipliers.raw_effects: Multiplies ore value by 1.08x every 0.2s for a total duration between 2.2s and 5.2s Average of 4.00x over 3.6s

astral predictor.how_to_use: The Astral Predictor is a that was added on August 7th, 2016. It was first unveiled on Berezaa's Twitter and his website. Astral Predictor upgrades ores by 1.08x every 0.2 seconds for a random amount of times between 11 or 26 times. It gives gradual upgrades over a short period of time. The amount of times the ore value is multiplied by 1.08x is dependent on a random number generator with the formula x+10, where x is an integer between 1 and 16. DurationMultiplier iterationTotal multiplier2.2s1.08^11~2.33x (min.)3.2s1.08^16~3.43x3.6s1.08^18~4.00x (avg.)4.2s1.08^21~5.03x5.2s1.08^26~7.39x (max.) To get ideal value out of this upgrader, it is best placed so that the ore is guaranteed to take more than 5.2 seconds before entering the furnace. The upgrading will still persist through any resetting device but replicator copies will not inherit the upgrading effect.
astral predictor.name: Astral Predictor

acquisition.source: Evolve with 12 Astral Predictors at The Craftsman.

astral setter.description: Takes significantly longer to kick in but is much more powerful than the Astral Predictor when it does.

extra.drawbacks: Does not upgrade immediately, can cause issues in 1-line rebirth setups
extra.effects: Multiplies ore value by 1.2x every 0.2s for a total duration between 2.2s to 5.2s
extra.multiplier_length: Minimum - x1.65 Maximum - x3.27 Average - x2.32
extra.old_effects: Before Reincarnation Update Multiplied ore value from x0.5 to x10 in 3 seconds to 3 minutes 20 seconds.
extra.old_source: Before Reincarnation Update Rebirth with 12 Astral Predictors on your base.
extra.source: Evolve with 12 Astral Predictors at The Craftsman.

multipliers.raw_effects: Multiplies ore value by 1.2x every 0.2s for a total duration between 2.2s to 5.2s

tags.source: Evolve with 12 Astral Predictors at The Craftsman.

astral setter.how_to_use: The Astral Setter is an Evolved Reborn-tier upgrader, successor of the Astral Predictor. It was added on 19 September, 2017 in the Resurrection Update. It was first revealed during Berezaa's YouTube Stream on 23 August, 2017. The Astral Setter upgraders like its predecessor, multiplying ore value over time, dependent on a random number generator, but with a buffed multiplier of 1.2x. The probability for each outcome listed below is the same. DurationMultiplier iterationTotal multiplierUpgrade Count2.2s1.2^11~7.43x5.53.2s1.2^16~18.5x83.6s1.2^18~26.6x94.2s1.2^21~46.0x10.55.2s1.2^26~114.5x13 It is recommended to put this item near the beginning of your setup, as well as long before or after any Education-giving upgraders so that ores have all the time they need in order to receive the full upgrade. Like its predecessor, the upgrading will still persist through any resetting device, but replicator copies will not inherit the upgrading effect.
astral setter.name: Astral Setter

atlantic monolith.description: Miles off the coast of the Haven, a diver found ancient underwater ruins. Someone then thought "Hey lemme put a conveyor through that!

extra.effects: Multiplies ore value by 4x Applies wet status effect for 5s Extinguishes flames Turns ore blue + glass material

multipliers.raw_effects: Multiplies ore value by 4x Applies wet status effect for 5s Extinguishes flames Turns ore blue + glass material

atlantic monolith.how_to_use: The Atlantic Monolith is a first shown in this tweet by OutOfOrderFoxy. It was added in the Reincarnation Update. The Atlantic Monolith has an additional function in addition to its 4x multiplier, which is that it also gives ore the wet status effect, which is a temporary variant of the fireproof status effect. This property lasts for 5 seconds, and ores are turned a shade of blue to reflect this change and has their material changed to glass.
atlantic monolith.name: Atlantic Monolith

Along with its increased multiplier of 13x, it also has an increased duration of the wet status effect (fire immunity) that it gives to ores, giving them immunity from fire for 7 seconds instead of 5.

atomium mine.how_to_use: The Atomium Mine is a added on 7th April, 2016. The Atomium Mine is a small dropper with very valuable ore, as well as a fast drop rate. In the Reincarnation Update, this received a unique gimmick; its ores receive gradual upgrades for a short period of time when the ore receives sparkles. Each upgrade from the sparkling effect has a chance to destroy the ore, however, it is not stackable with Resetting Devices. Splitting Atomium's ore with an Ore Sawmill or an Ore Chainsaw will result in an explosion, resulting in a destructive drawback. DurationMultiplier iterationTotal multiplier5sx1.05^20~2.65x7.5sx1.05^30~4.32x10sx1.05^40~7.04x (Item requires further testing)

atomyke mine.how_to_use: The Atomyke Mine is an , successor to the Atomium Mine. It was added in the Reincarnation Update. It serves as a direct buff to its predecessor, dropping ore that are worth, on average, 20x as much as its ore dropped by its predecessor. It also carries over its unique gimmick of receiving gradual upgrades from sparkles, which have a small chance to destroy the ore. Passing Atomyke ore through an Ore Sawmill will cause an explosion. aurora borealis.how_to_use: The Aurora Borealis is an , successor to the Northern Lights, added in the Resurrection Update. It was first revealed during Berezaa's YouTube Stream on 23rd August, 2017. It has a base multiplier of x0. In order to increase the multiplier, the furnace requires a source of sparkling coal ore, for which every piece that is processed, the multiplier is increased by +625. The multiplier caps at x21,875, or 35 pieces of sparkling coal ore. Its charge decay also takes a lot of ore to kick in, needing 5750 ores to be processed for -1 charge. Tips Using a Flaming Schrodinger or Vampire Spore can make the coal sparkle to power this furnace. The Angel's Blessing also works and much easier to use. Santa's Nice List adds sparkle effect on ores often, which is also an option for this furnace. Category:Furnace Category:The Resurrection Update Category:Large Category:Reborn-Proof Category:Multiplier-Changing Furnace Category:Fueled Category:Advanced Evolution Category:Superstitious Requirement

Auroral Metropolis upgrades ores differently whether they are frozen or not. For ores that do not have the frozen effect, they receive a 10x upgrade. However, for the ores that have the frozen effect, they receive a 25x upgrade. autumn breeze.how_to_use: The Autumn Breeze is an added in the 2016 September Update. It drops ore worth , every time an ore drops from this mine, a leaf from the top disappears, and it stops working once all leaves are gone, and must be replaced. only one ore can be dropped until it get absorbed or destroyed. The ores act as upgraders, doing x1.5 to an ore it touches and consuming it, allowing for another ore to be dropped. It can upgrade an ore 8 times, which leads to around a ~x25.6 total. basic iron mine.how_to_use: The Basic Iron Mine is a . It is one of the starter items that new Miner's Haven players obtain upon joining, alongside the Basic Furnace and 10 Basic Conveyors. When purchasing 99 Basic Iron Mines at once, there is a 1/500 chance to receive a Bazingium Mine.

basic ore purifier.how_to_use: The Basic Ore Purifier is a . It adds to all ores regardless of value, but any ores worth less than have an additional 1.1x multiplier. Despite the percentage upgrade this item has, it is still best used before other looping upgraders such as the Large Ore Upgrader.

basic research center.how_to_use: The Basic Research Center is a . It is the first Research Center you can buy and it gives 25 points for every 25 ores processed in it. (1 point per ore.)

bazingium mine.how_to_use: The Bazingium Mine is a dropper added in the Fifth Anniversary Event. The ores it produces are flung, similar to a cannon, a random distance away from the dropper. This mine is essentially useless practically and should only be used as a decoration.

extra.submission_description: Made with TheBlueMac55, we had an absolute blast making this awesome item! Intended as an enchant or summer exotic. Intended to work insanely well during day, poorly during night. You wouldn't want to find this in your grandma's garden.
extra.submission_item_effects: Works better during the day

behemoth blossom.how_to_use: The Behemoth Blossom is a Slipstream-tier upgrader added in the Reincarnation Update. It multiplies the ore value depending on its upgrade counter, having either a multiplier of 100x for ores with less than 20 upgrades, or 60x for ores with 20 upgrades or more. In both cases, the ore has its size increased which can make it difficult to use with other upgraders. A Hydraulic Presser or an Ore Sawmill can come in handy.
behemoth blossom.name: Behemoth Blossom

behemoth snowflake.how_to_use: The Behemoth Snowflake is a obtained in Part III of the Christmas Update 2019 by opening the Shockingly Freezing Present. It upgrades ore by x3 once, and applies a frozen effect, similar to the Permafrost Refiner. It can be used to unlock the Crystal Shrine. The slippery (frozen) effect can be removed with a Blazing Annihilator. Even though this makes ore "cold" and slippery, they aren't fireproof, as the frozen effect no longer provides fireproofing as of the Reincarnation Update.

berezium mine.description: Named after the scientist that discovered it, berezium mine is worth a ton of money. However, the mine is MASSIVE.
berezium mine.how_to_use: The Berezium Mine is a . It was named after Berezaa, the creator of Miner's Haven, added in the Initial Release. It was removed in The Resurrection Update, and was replaced by the Emerald Mine. Later, the item was re-added in the Halloween 2019 Event as one of the items sold by the Headless Baron. It also made a return in the Halloween 2020 Event, being sold by The Masked Man.
berezium mine.name: Berezium Mine

big alberto.description: A device used by the D.U.C during the galactic war against the Dracoherds. This device blasted gamma rays, reducing their fleets to nothing but space debris.

big alberto.how_to_use: Big Alberto is an that was added in The Trinity Update. It was confirmed in a tweet by the Miner's Haven News Twitter account. Big Alberto features two upgraders: one at the bottom on its conveyor and another suspended above it. The upgrade above multiplies ore value by 25x while the bottom one multiplies ore value by 10x. Only one of these upgrades can be applied. It is recommended that you try to get the top upgrade. To achieve this, use ore cannons or related items to reach the upgrader. The light beam is not the upgrader; the ore must touch the circular ring above the bottom upgrader in order to receive the 25x upgrade. Category:Upgrader Category:Advanced Reborn Category:Rebirth Category:Reborn-Proof Category:The Trinity Update Category:Large Category:Length 6 Upgrader
big alberto.name: Big Alberto

Surviving ores are set on fire, destroying them after 4s, if they are not extinguished. big bertha.how_to_use: The Big Bertha is a that was added in the Secret Update and is one of three (supposed to be four) secret reborn items that were added during this update. It multiplies ore value by x10, but requires ore be inserted in such a way that in enters the purple hole at the front side. This can be achieved by using a mine that drops directly into it or an Ore Cannon, an alternative method would be using a Hydraulic Unstable Conveyor raised by 1 to launch the ore in.

blood magic.how_to_use: The Blood Magic is an that was released in the Halloween Event 2016. It processes ore value at a multiplier of 20x. It also features a counter on the bigger of the two islands, which keeps count of the number of processed ore. When this number reaches 99, the next ore is processed at 300x and a copy of the ore is produced from the Mage's Staff. This ore can be caught with conveyors and processed, also with a 300x multiplier. (Formerly made 5 copies.) The model of Lucifer present at the back does a dab animation when the 100th ore is processed, as well as producing a bass audio.

boomite mine.how_to_use: The Boomite Mine is a based off the Azure Mines ore of the same name. It was announced on Berezaa's stream on 11/10/2016. This dropper will consume coal, giving it x10 with a limit of , but also causing an explosion, with a 1/8 chance to destroy the ore. There can only be 4 Boomite ores per mine on your base at a time. Boomite ore has a special red flame on it that prevents it from being destroyed by fire similarly to the Symmetrium Mine. Extinguishing this flame will both allow the ore to be burned and remove its coal upgrade effect. If the mine is withdrawn, the ores it has left behind will be stripped of their unique effects; this means they will not explode when coming in contact with coal ore. This bug also appears with several other items, such as Hades' Fang. The special flame effect emit from the ore can not be removed by the Wild Spore, Deadly Spore, or the Azure Spore (as it is not a considered a negative effect). The Azure Refiner can destroy this ore. The ore replicator allows you to have more than 4 boomite ores on your base. However, the mine stops working until all of the boomite ores are gone. When duplicated by the Ore Replicator, the ore that come out have a fire effect. However, this effect does not destroy the ore.
boomite mine.name: Boomite Mine

breached motherboard.how_to_use: The Breached Motherboard is an Evolved Reborn-tier furnace added in the Reincarnation Update. The Breached Motherboard works similarly to its predecessor, Invasive Cyberlord, in that it has 3 upgrade beams above the furnace, each multiplying ore value by x2 for a total multiplier of x8. With an initial x500 multiplier, if all beams are hit, this furnace will process ores at a multiplier of x4000. Appearance It looks like the Invasive Cyberlord but bigger, has more monitors and servers and seems to have a circuitry effect branching out. There is also an antenna on either side.
breached motherboard.name: Breached Motherboard

breech loader.description: A crude device which violently tears ores from other dimensions into our own. Ores have a slight chance to randomly duplicate.

extra.effects: Ores have a small chance to replicate themselves
extra.other_notes: Ore has 0.2 (20%) transparency.
extra.submission_description: "We ran out of practical applications for death lasers so we made one into a mine. #YOLO"
extra.submission_item_effects: Drops bursts of 3-5 ore every 2 seconds. 1st and 5th ores are $1B, 2nd and 4th ores are $1.5B and 3rd ore is $2B. Works with cells.

multipliers.raw_effects: Ores have a small chance to replicate themselves

breech loader.how_to_use: The Breech Loader is a that was first unveiled on Berezaa's Twitter and was explained on Berezaa's website. The Breech Loader is a fairly rare dropper with high ore value and drop rate, as well as having a unique gimmick where its ores have a small random chance to replicate themselves. A small drawback is its rotation of 45 degrees, requiring some thought during placement since most droppers are usually facing in a straight direction.
breech loader.name: Breech Loader

bubonic plague.how_to_use: The Bubonic Plague was an that was originally added in Part I of the Halloween 2020 Event. It multiplies ore value by x15 alongside applying Plague. Plague will increase the ore size after 5 seconds, increasing by 10% of its current size up to 15 times, at which point it will destroy it. Only the Ghosdeeri's AntidoteOnly if the 5 seconds period is still up. and Plague Doctor can remove this effect. If the ore has been upgraded by Airborne Pathogenic Spewer, it will instead apply UberPlague. It has the same properties as the Plague; however, whenever an ore is destroyed with UberPLagueOnly if the ore dies by Plague and not from any other sources., it will infect other nearby ores with the Plague. If an ore with the Plague effect is processed in the Plagued Forest, its regular processing rate of x20 turns into x75.
bubonic plague.name: Bubonic Plague

candy center.how_to_use: The Candy Center is a added in the Halloween Event 2019. It is used to generate candy with ores from the Swintite Mine, its Super variant, and Pumpkinite Mine. More candy is generated per ore depending on how many candy upgraders you use before processing the ore. To collect candy, you press the green button on the opposite side of the processing part and by clicking ‘Yes’ on the prompt, you will be given the candy currently held inside. (The button is not shown in the picture.)

candy factory.how_to_use: The Candy Factory is a added in the 2020 Halloween Event. The furnace can store up to 10,000 Candy at once before needing to be manually collected. It also increases the value of any Candy ore that enter it by 25%. It also processes normal ores by x25. Upon pressing the button to empty the furnace, a GUI will pop up asking to confirm whether or not you would like to empty the furnace. In order to counter being AFK, the position of the yes button will change occasionally when it is pressed. candy metropolis.how_to_use: The Candy Metropolis is an added in Part II of the 2020 Halloween Event. This furnace acts like Candy Center and Candy Factory, but has a bigger cap of at once before needing to be manually collected. Upon pressing the button to empty the furnace, a GUI will pop up asking to confirm whether or not you would like to empty the furnace. In order to counter auto-clickers, the position of the yes button will swap places with the no button after a certain number of button presses. The candy collection button itself is at an odd angle to further impede the use of auto-clicking. It is highly recommended to get this before any gift. carrot village.how_to_use: The is a added during the 2024 Easter Event. It features two upgrader parts with different effects. The bottom upgrade part multiplies ore value by x4 and decreases their size by 0.3 studs on all axes. It also requires you to use a Standard Platform to reach it. The top part increases an ore's size by 0.3 studs on all axes and multiplies ore value by x6 if they did not hit the bottom upgrader first. If the bottom upgrader was hit first, it will multiply ore value by x9. Both upgrader parts can be used with each other without a Resetter.

catalyzed star.how_to_use: The Catalyzed Star is a added on March 8th, 2017 and was first revealed on Berezaa's Twitter. The Catalyzed Star serves as a hybrid of both a limitless and a money-capped upgrader. Its first upgrade multiplies ore value by 5x with no additional effects and can be amplified up to 20x with the previous use of a Nova Star, as well as its fusion, the Ore Nova, a pulsar-type item. Subsequent upgrades to ores will have drastically lower multipliers, but will continue up to a cap of $1NvD, with the multipliers getting even smaller as the ore gets more valuable. The multipliers are shown below: Value ThresholdMultiplier1.6x1.3x1.15x1.06x It takes 88 upgrades to go from 10DD to 100QnD, 66 upgrades to go from 100QnD to 1SpD, and 237 upgrades to go from 1SpD to 1NvD, for a total of 391 upgrades to go from 10DD to 1NvD. Therefore, for a Catalyzed Star loop that takes about 4 seconds per loop, it will take about 26 minutes to bring your ore value from 10DD to 1NvD. Any upgrade applied to the ore past the 1st makes the ore incompatible with the Black Dwarf and its evolved variant, the Void Star. Appearance The Catalyzed Star is wider than both The Catalyst and the Morning Star. The neon red-orange of the upgrade beam probably originates from the combination of The Catalyst's red and the Morning Star's yellow as their signature colors. The Catalyzed Star has a fire on the two corners of each end, whereas the Morning Star has one fire on its bottom right corner. The Catalyzed Star's star also features more points than the Morning Star's.
catalyzed star.name: Catalyzed Star

cell collider.how_to_use: The Cell Collider is a that was released on June 10th 2017 in the Second Sacrifice Update. The Cell Collider has been the first shop cell furnace to be released since 2015 (with the release of the Cell Particalizer) and takes its place as the second most powerful shop cell furnace. The Cell Collider is one of the only cell furnaces with all four sides of the furnace closed, meaning many droppers are unable to drop ores directly into the furnace. Players in many cases would have to launch the ore with an Ore Cannon or Ore Collider and a wall behind the furnace.

clover rune.how_to_use: The Clover Rune is a Collectible-tier upgrader that was released during the Magic Clover Event. It returned during the St. Patrick's Day 2020 event. It multiplies ore value by 1 + (1/x), where x is the nth time that the ore is upgraded by the upgrader. That means the first upgrade is x2, the second is 1 + 1/2 = x3/2, etc. However, ores can only be upgraded up to 4 times with this item, resulting in a maximum of x5 to ore value without resets. Resetting devices will also reset how the multiplier is counted. coal mine.how_to_use: The Coal Mine is a Common-tier utility dropper that produces coal ore worth , which can be used to power industrial machines (such as the Industrial Ruby Mine and the Industrial Ore Welder). It is often used in that manner, often having the coal travel on conveyor arrays heading to an industrial input. It is also useable for some items if ores are modified, such as sparkling coal ores can power up Northern Lights. A premium-tier variant, the Triple Coal Mine,and its seasonal variant, the Spooky Coal Mine, produce triple the coal in the same time span while occupying the same hitbox space of 2x2 units. This is why the Coal Mine is only used for very early lives, as the Triple Coal Mine is a very common item gotten from mystery boxes and they can stay with user until a players decided to perform The Ultimate Sacrifice, The Second Sacrifice, and the upcoming Third Sacrifice. One slight issue, due to the potential for impossibly high multipliers on upgrade-based furnaces, such as the Sage Redeemer, coal ore seems to have a 'time out' where it despawns. This happens when going through some upgraders, or over an as of yet indeterminate time. Category:Common Category:Dropper Category:Shop Item Category:Coal Category:Utility Category:Small Category:Miner's Haven Release

contraland.how_to_use: The CONTRALAND is a only available from at a 0.5% chance, added in the 5th Anniversary Event. When an ore passes through its value is multiplied by 11x if The Masked Man is not present, and 15x if he is. In addition to this, the upgrader plays the signature cackle of the Masked Man. The ore also has a random effect applied to it, as shown below: EffectsChancesSmoking1/3SparklingChange in volume The way the ore has its volume changed is dependent on the size of the ore before it entered. If the size of the ore is either below or above 1x1x1, then the size of the ore is set to the standard 1x1x1. On the contrary, if the ore is indeed 1x1x1 in size when it enters the upgrade beam, then the ore has its size instead changed to 3x3x3.
contraland.name: CONTRALAND

cooling chamber.how_to_use: The Cooling Chamber is a Reborn-tier upgrader added in Part II of the 2020 Saint Patrick's Day Event. It's a very unique item as it serves as an upgrader with heavy multipliers and a fire extinguisher. However, it has to be maintained in order for it to work at maximum power. It holds an internal variable that determines whether it is neutral or overheated. This variable starts at 36 and can increase or decrease depending on the ores that pass through it. This variable does not have an upper nor a lower limit. Status effect +/- to variableFlaming-3Non-flaming-1Frozen+3 If the Cooling Chamber's internal variable reaches 0, it will become overheated (visible when the lights on the upgrader turn from white to orange). In order to revert the overheat status, the Cooling Chamber requires its internal variable to reach 18, equivalent to 6 frozen ores. The Cooling Chamber cannot be maintained solely by frozen, flaming ores. If the Cooling Chamber is neutral, then the flaming ore's -3 takes priority, and vice versa for the overheated status. (Frozen ores are not the same as fireproof ores. Frozen ores are slippery, but fireproof ores cannot be set on fire.)
cooling chamber.name: Cooling Chamber

crystal altar.how_to_use: The Crystal Altar is an that was added during Part I of the Christmas Event 2019. It is the evolution of the Crystal Shrine, and like its predecessor, it requires a complex combination of inputs to unlock its power. Sealing & Unsealing Process When placed, the Crystal Altar is initially sealed, and requires a series of inputs to unseal in the form of ores with specific effects. Each correct input will advance the unsealing processing into the next stage, changing both the color and name of the Crystal Altar. Ores used to unseal the Crystal Altar require a value of at least $1qd to properly work; otherwise, the ore will be rejected. If an incorrect ore is submitted into the furnace, it will be rejected, shown with a red X. Incorrect steps do not reset the seal or alter the current stage. Like its predecessor, the Crystal Altar will emit tremor sounds when it changes states, and will emit a thunderous roar upon being unsealed. If an ore that was dropped from a Noobite Mine is processed into the Crystal Altar, it will cause the Crystal Altar to become cursed, resulting in a processing rate of x1. +Unsealing StepsStageStateColorOre requirementExample item1SealedLavenderFlaming ore2FlamingRedFireproof ore3FrozenYellowSmoking ore4BrokenBlueSparkling ore5ShinyGreenRadioactive ore6PoisonedIndigoEducated ore(ore does not have to be educated)7UnsealedWhiteN/A
crystal altar.name: Crystal Altar

It also does not need to be maintained constantly, so it only needs to be unsealed if withdrawn and placed down again. When unlocking the next stage, a tremor can be heard. dark illuminator.how_to_use: The Dark Illuminator is a furnace, a fusion between the Ore Illuminator and the Dark Magic, added on Part 2 of the 2017 Summer Update. It functions as a buffed version of the Dark Magic. Instead of the usual 12x and 45x while activated, it has a normal 500x multiplier and a 5,000x multiplier while activated. It also processes ores at an additional 5,000 multiplier (5,500x for deactivated and 10,000x for activated) if the ore processed has been upgraded by any illuminator item. Ores processed while the furnace is not activated have their processed value shown in grey colour; a sharp green shade is used for ores processed while the furnace is activated. The first remote click is delayed for 1 second until the Dark Illuminator activates. Every click adds 0.7 seconds to its activation timer. The furnace's size is also slightly smaller than its lesser counterpart, the Dark Magic, by 1 unit lengthwise. Appearance This item seems to have more influence from Dark Magic rather than Ore Illuminator. The item is different from Dark Magic as the walls on the furnace part have been replaced with green triangles and the crystals on the Dark Illuminator are green (to show influence from Ore Illuminator). It also has Illuminati particles coming from the triangles, which you can barely see in the picture.
dark illuminator.name: Dark Illuminator

dark magic.how_to_use: The Dark Magic is a furnace added in the 2015 November Updates. It is a remotely activated furnace, in fact, it is the first button-activated furnace. This is a great furnace to use if you get it early, having a potentially higher multiplier than that of the Dreamer's Might.

diamond breech loader.how_to_use: The Diamond Breech Loader is a , first revealed on Berezaa's Twitter in a Tweet that was deleted the very next day, added in the Ultimate Update. The Diamond Breech Loader is a cross between the notoriously ineffective Massive Diamond Mine and the Breech Loader. It holds the status of having an incredibly high raw ore value and a fast drop rate, mining ludicrously expensive ores valued at . Likewise with the Breech Loader, part of the item is at an angle, but not diagonally. Instead, only the dropper segment is slanted forward. This is much more convenient and easier to use than the Breech Loader and its diagonal rotation. Its ore size is a little bigger than the Breech Loader's ore size, making it tough to use in upgraders where a small ore size is required, such as the Gingerbread House. Due to the ore falling from such a height, positioning this item correctly is required or it will collide with shorter items. Category:Reborn Fusion Category:Massive Dropper Category:Dropper Category:Large Category:Reborn-Proof Category:Negative Upgrade Counter Category:The Ultimate Update
diamond breech loader.name: Diamond Breech Loader

doom incarnate.how_to_use: The Doom Incarnate is a added during the Part 2 of the 2025 Halloween Event. It has 4 upgrade beams each having a base multiplier of x2, increased to x3.5 if the ore have a certain immunity with certain beam. Below is a list of effect each beam does. BeamEffectMortar5% chance to kill oresIf the ore has Poison Immunity, then it is replaced with Radioactive ImmunityTeslaApplies yellow sparklesIf ore has Radioactive Immunity, then it is replaced with Fire Immunity (Fireproof)FlamethrowerChanges ore material to CrackedLavaApply a non-destructible green fireIf the ore has Fire Immunity (Fireproof), then it is replaced with Frostbite immunityChainsawSubtracts 0.1 ore sizeIf the ore has Frostbite Immunity, then it is replaced with Poison Immunity If the ore has been upgraded by all four beams, then it applies Battle-Hardened tag, upgraders such as The Guillotine will always upgrade at its maximum multiplier of x45 whilst removing its kill chance. draconicglass mine.how_to_use: The Draconicglass Mine is an , successor to the Dragonglass Mine, that was added in the 2019 Halloween Event. The amount of ore dropped per click is random, ranging from 1 to 5 ores. Unlike the Dragonglass Mine, which increases ore value every burst, ore value increases with each ore, starting from and caps out at before resetting to $0. Like its predecessor, ores from this mine have a reduced chance of being destroyed by blasters. Upon use, the dropper may temporarily "rage", turning dropped ores and the mine red. Ores dropped during this time have a higher value.

draedon s gauntlet.how_to_use: The Draedon's Gauntlet is a first revealed and given to several well-known contributors, then fully released in the Reincarnation Update. It goes through 2 phases. It first multiplies ore value by a massive 250x, then multiplies ore value by 1.05x 50 times over the course of 10 seconds, resulting in a total multiplier of roughly 2867x. A drawback, shared with the Ore Illuminator, is that the ore cannot have its upgrade counter changed while it is being upgraded. However, 0 Upgrade Counter upgraders may still be used during this. The Ore Hypernova, together with the Gargantium Core and the Gargantium Mine, can be used to let ores instantly receive the total multiplier of the Draedon's Gauntlet, as well as a bonus x1.05, totaling to a x3010 upgrade. Before Release Before this item was released to all players in the Reincarnation Update, it previously had a much higher total multiplier of around x1.225T. This was the result of the ore value being multiplied by 1.25x 125 times, as shown in this video. After being given to several select players, it was presumably nerfed to only have a 2860x total multiplier and then had its stats changed once again in the Reincarnation Update to its current stats. Recipe |-|Recipe 1 = Item NameQuantity Needed1174 |-|Recipe 2= Item NameQuantity Needed1131 |-|Recipe 3= Item NameQuantity Needed85431
draedon s gauntlet.name: Draedon's Gauntlet

dragon cannon.how_to_use: The Dragon Cannon is an upgrader cannon, a fusion between the Railgun Cannon and the Dragon Blaster, added in the Fifth Anniversary Event. It is functionally identical to its predecessor, the Railgun Cannon, but with the addition of a multiplier of x15 and a variable velocity dependent on the number of dragon-esque items used on the ore before the cannon. The velocity of the Dragon Cannon when used without Dragon blasters is already slightly higher than the Railgun Cannon, reaching a further 10 units. For each Machine tag from either a Hydra Blaster or Dragonglass Blaster, the velocity of the Dragon Cannon is incrementally increased by 10 units. This means this cannot be a instant replacement for the railgun cannon, as this extra length results in a slightly different curve when the ore is ejected. This can cause problems in your setup, and you may need to remake an upgrader line.
dragon cannon.name: Dragon Cannon

dragonglass blaster.how_to_use: The Dragonglass Blaster is an , a fusion between the Dragon Blaster and the Dragonglass Mine, added in The Ultimate Update. It was first unveiled on Berezaa's Twitter as one of the images. It upgrades ore with a constant multiplier of 9x, with a 10% chance to both explode and destroy ore that passes through it while turned on. The more clicks the remote receives, the longer the item is turned on, similarly to the Dragonglass Mine. One click is enough to leave this item turned on for 3 seconds before shutting off, unlike the Dragonglass Mine's delay. Appearance It has the body of the Dragon Blaster but also has an extra pair of smaller wings, crystals taking the place of the dragon's teeth, and a receiver on its back to represent how the item is influenced by the Dragonglass Mine. The Dragonglass Blaster does not spit any fire and the crystals along with its wings are blue when it's deactivated, but turns purple and starts spitting fire when it is activated.
dragonglass blaster.name: Dragonglass Blaster

dreamcatcher.how_to_use: The Dreamcatcher is a added in the Skipped Life Update, on 31st March, 2017. It has a base multiplier of x7 which increases by +1 for every sparkling ore that is processed, up to a max of x52. The multiplier decays by -1 every 10 seconds. In addition to the processing multiplier, every 10 charge will produce a beam from the dream catcher model present at the top of the item downwards. This beam multiplies ore value by 7x and gives sparkles, meaning with a minimum charge of 10, the furnace is easier to maintain. Sparkling ore that is processed in the Dreamcatcher receive a bonus x1.25. All things considered, the Dreamcatcher has a maximum potential of x455. Considering the beams it produces, it is possible to use the Dreamcatcher as an unconventional portable upgrader.
dreamcatcher.name: Dreamcatcher

Berezaa thought that the Dreamer's Anguish was overpowered, so he nerfed it in the Skipped Life Update. The max possible multiplier was nerfed from x1Qn to x80qd. dreamer s birthday cake.how_to_use: The is an added during the Ninth Anniversary Event. This upgrader has 5 possible different effects depending on the vertical layer that ore passes by. An ore can only receive one of each effect without a resetter. Effect are listed on this table are from top to bottom. LayerEffectMightx2, gives ore sparkles.Sightx1.5.Blightx1.5, removes fire, poison, and radioactive.Frightx1.4, and duplicates the ore up to 5 times.Anguishx1.75, 10% chance to explode ore. (can't kill ore)Maximum multiplier of x11 Note that it can't upgrade ores at ground level, so anti-gravity is useful. Also note that duplicated ores don't retain the momentum of the original, so launching ores via means like Ore Cannons might cause you to lose the extra ores if you aren't careful.

dreamer s blight.how_to_use: The Dreamer's Blight is an Advanced Reborn-tier furnace added in the Reincarnation Update. It has a base multiplier of x500. The multiplier can be increased if the ore was upgraded by spore-related items, as shown in the table below: ItemMultiplier increase+250+500 It also increases the multiplier of spore-related upgraders just by having the furnace placed down: ItemUsual multiplierNew multiplier x7x10x11x14x25/x50x28/x56x5-x22x26 However, as time passes, the Dreamer's Blight's flora slowly decays. After 500 seconds, the Dreamer's Blight becomes fully decayed, no longer providing buffs to other upgraders; its process multiplier will continue to decay regardless. This can be remedied by replacing the item. Ores do not have to be processed by the furnace in order to get the increased upgrade from spore related items, making it very useful to still have it place down if another furnace is used.
dreamer s blight.name: Dreamer's Blight

dreamer s devastation.description: The Dreamers could only dream for a short periods of time, as their dreams would always be tainted by Pandora's blight. dreamer s fright.how_to_use: The Dreamer's Fright is a that was released in July 2015, one month after the game's initial release. The Dreamer's Fright has erratic stats as it can add or subtract value and may process ore value by a negative multiplier. It first adds or subtracts up to , then multiplies the ore value by a number between -5 and 45. To prevent players from acquiring negative money, their cash is set to . Additionally, ores must be worth at least in order to process, due to the furnace subtracting from the ore value. The Dreamer's Fright's leaves fall and act as ores, but are not destroyed when they touch the baseplate. The initial value of the leaves is anywhere from to . These leaves tend to act more of an annoyance than actual ore as they can cause lag, as well as spill ore around the base.
dreamer s fright.name: Dreamer's Fright

The Dreamer's Life has a high base multiplier of x125 which can be additively increased by +50 for each Dreamer's item placed down on your base. Both a Reborn-tier Dreamer's item and its Evolved successor stack. The following Dreamer items (and their shiny counterparts) are able to boost the multiplier: Normal and Shiny variants of the same item are counted as 2 different items for this multiplier Having 8 unique Dreamer's items placed down will result in a maximum multiplier of x525.

easter statues.how_to_use: The Easter Statues 🗿 is an that could be acquired in Part III of the Christmas Update 2019 and Part II of the Winter Event 2020 by unwrapping the Gift of Funny Eggs 🥚. Its top upgrader does a x3 upgrade to ore, and if the ore then used the bottom upgrader (provided the ore has gone through the top upgrader already), a x6 multiplier is applied, a total of a x18 upgrade. However, if the ore uses the bottom upgrader first or the bottom upgrader only, no upgrade will be applied to the ore. This upgrader is especially unique mainly from the S-Shaped Way-Up-High upgrader that runs perpendicular to the main conveyor, which has shadows leading into total darkness like the Schrodinger Evaluator.

electronic furnace.how_to_use: The Electronic Furnace is a released in The Resurrection Update. It was first revealed during Berezaa's YouTube Stream on August 23rd, 2017. It is a manually activated furnace, requiring you to push the buttons to power it, similar to excavators. When the furnace is unactivated, the furnace processes ores by 1x their value and acts like a Basic Furnace. The text that appears on the furnace to show you how much money the ore was worth is gray. Otherwise, the lights on the furnace turn on and the furnace processors ore by 20x their value. To turn it on, you have to press the button 4 times and will last 2 seconds. When this happens, the text that appears above the furnace is blue. Due to the conveyor in front of it, players will have to be careful about what upgraders they place before the furnace or they will have to place a conveyor before it, due to the fact that bigger items will have interfering hitboxes that block the button. When the furnace is activated, one ore will count as 2 on the ore counter for getting research points. This means that instead of 80 ores, you would only need 40 ores to get RP from the furnace. (Assuming all the ores were processed while the furnace was activated)
electronic furnace.name: Electronic Furnace

elevated furnace.how_to_use: The Elevated Furnace is a that was released in the Summer Update of 2017. Ores have to reach the height of the Elevated Furnace with either the Ore Elevator, the Large Conveyor Ramp, or even the Ore Cannon (and even more). It processes the ore at 5x. The furnace is blocked on three sides, but open on one. This furnace is good if you're in the late millions. But, once you reach the billions, it would be more convenient to use the Heavenly Forge. It doesn't have any elevation required, so it is easier to get ores into.
elevated furnace.name: Elevated Furnace

empyrean monument.how_to_use: The Empyrean Monument is an upgrader added in the New Heights Update. It has a x15 multiplier while placed on the ground and increases by x15 per elevation, to a max of x150 at 10 elevations. For reference, a Standard or Mega Platform is 8 elevations high. It features a rather unconventional upgrade part that floats above the conveyor, requiring the user to think of a method in order to get their ore to effectively touch the beam. This can be achieved in a few ways, including hydraulic conveyors or cannons. In addition, the conveyor's walls squeeze inwards, disabling large ores from passing through.
empyrean monument.name: Empyrean Monument

enchanted library.how_to_use: The Enchanted Library is a that was added in The Trinity Update. It is linked to the God of Knowledge, Magnar. It has a base multiplier of x65 which increases by +1 for each second since the ore was produced, up to a cap of x200. Using an upgrader associated with time, or ore life, will "skip" 5 seconds (effectively adding a +x5 to the multiplier). Along with an extra effect from the following table, all with equal chances, but mutually exclusive (ore cannot obtain more than one effect per use). Time Upgraders Click the expand button to see the list. Item*Shiny versions of items are also buffed. Extra Effects Click the expand button to see the list. Special EffectProbabilityWet (6 seconds)1/9FireproofGreen SparklesGradual x1.8 upgrade*Instant Education**Poison ImmunityRadiation ImmunitySmokeFrostbite Immunity *Gradual x1.8 upgrade, upgrades by x1.03 every 0.5 seconds 20 times. Does not apply additional upgrade counter. **Instant Education, skips the education effect of the Ore Illuminator and any of its related items. This is identical to the behaviour of the Gargantium Mine with the Stardust Illuminator.

equinox.how_to_use: Equinox is an that was released during the Ultimate Sacrifice update, alongside with the comeback of The Beehive. The Equinox multiplies every ore that enters by 70x until there are 7 ores. The 5th, 6th, and 7th ores are processed. When the 7th ore is processed, all the ores that have been turned dark disappear, ready for the next batch. According to the stats, the max multiplier of the Equinox is x4900, assuming your 7th ore is the ore you intend to process. Ores that have previously been upgraded, no matter how many times, can also be processed, making this item a potentially powerful cell furnace to use in end-game setups. The only real drawback of this item is the size of ore you use, and your ore limit. Prior to the Easter update on April 15th, 2017, the Equinox previously multiplied all ores by x10, started processing beyond the 6th ore and ores did not disappear. This did mean a max multiplier of x2500 was possible assuming a player has maxed out their ore limit.

eternal fracture.how_to_use: The Eternal Fracture is a , a fusion between the Eternal Journey and The Fracture, added in The Ultimate Update. It raises the value of the ore to the power of 1.14, then processes at x2B. However, it has a drawback: the ore is held in stasis while it is processed for 1 to 8 seconds. Its hitbox allows most of the mines to be able to be directly dropped into the furnace part of the item, allowing for easy use without the requirement of an Ore Cannon. eternal journey.how_to_use: The Eternal Journey is a Reborn-tier furnace. It was added on April 21st, 2016. This item was first unveiled in Berezaa's developer stream, the same day it was added to Miner's Haven. Eternal Journey was the first Reborn-tier item to be exclusively available to those over life 100. Although this furnace can process ores at 100x its value, it has a major disadvantage: the ore that enters the furnace turns black and sits for around 1–15 seconds, and the furnace also turns black (an indication that the ore is being processed). Any ore that enters the furnace while it's black will be destroyed instead of processed. This furnace is recommended for processing ONE very valuable ore, so it would be smart to use a mine like the Hades' Fang on a Reversible Conveyor directly before the furnace. After all ores are condensed into one valuable one, you switch the reversible conveyor and process that ore. Alternatively, use another furnace that is not able to accidentally destroy ores, as the choice of furnace does not have significant multipliers in the grand scheme of things. Category:Furnace Category:Rebirth Category:Reborn Category:Large Category:Reborn-Proof Category:April 2016 Updates
eternal journey.name: Eternal Journey

eternal limbo.how_to_use: The Eternal Limbo is the , the successor to the Eternal Journey, added in Black Friday 2019. When ore reaches this furnace, they will sit around for exactly thirty seconds before they are fully processed for 19,000x their value. (Ores with effects like poison and fire will still die in this time.) The Void The Eternal Limbo acts as the primary access point to The Void. Three mazes set within the realm can be accessible by combining ore from The Daegelart with an upgrade tag from a maze's associated upgrader, before having the ore get processed by the Limbo. The associated upgrader are here as follows: Daegel's maze - (leads to the Void Drive) Heart of The Void - (leads to The Forbidden Tome) For access to the Shiny Void, a shiny version of this item is required. In addition, the furnace must process ore from a that has been upgraded by a . Appearance The monochrome tree has grown larger, and the front has an open gate. An hourglass is to the right of the tree, and the stairs are much wider at the base. A visual effect of the furnace is that the ores will travel up the stairs as they both fade into nothingness.
eternal limbo.name: Eternal Limbo

excalibur.how_to_use: The Excalibur is an Exotic-tier furnace that was exclusive to May 2016 and was introduced in the Outdoors Update. Unlike the more straightforward furnaces in Miner's Haven, this does not multiply ore value by a specific value. Instead, Excalibur has a unique effect where it stacks value from ore previously processed by the furnace with other ores processed at a max of 14 seconds after it. By processing a high-value ore and then putting ores after it, all of the ores are now worth the same as the aforementioned high-value ore you inserted into the furnace. Droppers with high production speed prove the most useful when used with this furnace, i.e. Wubium Mine, Santa's Rage, or Atomium Mine.

father of freon.how_to_use: The Father of Freon is an added in the Reincarnation Update. It is one of the Trinity Upgraders, along with the Son of Poison and Spirit of Fire. The Father of Freon multiplies ore value by 9x, extinguishes any ore on fire, and applies a freezing effect. The freezing effect makes ore slippery and allows ore to be upgraded by some items like the Frigid Dystopia. If the Father of Freon is used as the last of the Trinity upgraders, its effects are amplified. When an ore makes contact with the upgrade beam its value is instead multiplied by 45x. In addition to the frozen effect, the ore is also given the frostbite effect as well. The frostbite effect is the polar opposite of fire, requiring flames in order to be removed. Otherwise, the ore is destroyed in 4 seconds.

flying dutchman.how_to_use: The Flying Dutchman is an furnace released in the 2016 Halloween Event. Every 30 minutes, if you submit 1000 well-upgraded ores, it will award you a free or a Lucky Clover. A well-upgraded ore is considered an ore with strictly more than 1 reflectance (i.e. more than 20 upgrader counter). For reference, the Overlord Device and the True Overlord Device both have 20 upgrader counter. The probability of getting a Regular Box after 30 minutes and 1000 ores is 6/7 (~85.7%) and for a Lucky Clover, 1/7 (~14.3%). The player is able to stack the ability of the Flying Dutchman with a max stack of 5; however, this used to not be the case until a little after the end of the 2019 Halloween Event.
flying dutchman.name: Flying Dutchman

fractured reality.how_to_use: The Fractured Reality is a added in the Shiny Patch update. The Fractured Reality multiplies ore value based on their status effect, with a base multiplier of 7x if the ore does not have any. Depending on the status effect, the multiplier can be increased up to 13x. However, the player is realistically looking at a 10x as the flaming status effect is prioritized the most. Also, the corresponding multiplier has its effect reversed. For example, if the ore has all three effects, then only the flames status effect is reversed. The table below lists the multiplier for each compatible status effect, as well as ordering them by prioritization (top least prioritized, bottom most prioritized): Status EffectsMultiplierExample ItemNonex7Sparklingx13(Sparkles are replaced with Smoking)Frozen x11 (Ores are no longer frozen)Flamingx10(Fire is replaced with Wet for 8 seconds)
fractured reality.name: Fractured Reality

freon bombarder.how_to_use: The Freon Bombarder is a fine-point upgrader and one of the first Luxury-tier items in the game. It makes ores fireproof, not allowing the ore to be set on fire no matter how many igniting items the ore goes through. The Freon Bombarder also makes ores frozen. Some upgraders require the ores to be frozen in order to have an effect, such as the Frigid Dystopia, Turbine Chamber, and Cooling Chamber. Even though there are sprays on the upgrader, the ore must hit the center beam to become fireproof and frozen.
freon bombarder.name: Freon Bombarder

garden of gaia.how_to_use: The Garden of Gaia is a added in The Trinity Update. It is linked to the goddess of nature, Gaia. The Garden of Gaia's basic attributes are a 90x multiplier. Its unique feature, however, are the green particles visible on ores after passing through. These particles have been nicknamed Gaia's Blessing. Gaia's Blessing will multiply the ore's value by x1.02 every time a new machine tag is applied. This means almost all upgrades receive a 2% boost on top of its usual multiplier. This effect does not apply to ores that are on fire and Gaia's Blessing also only lasts 15 seconds. Gaia's Blessing is ignored by The Death Cap and the Azure Refiner, as well as its variants. It can only be removed via a resetter. Recipe |-|Recipe 1 = Item NameQuantity Needed27976 |-|Recipe 2 = Item NameQuantity Needed20662211 Red Giant and Morning Star are interchangable.
garden of gaia.name: Garden of Gaia

gargantium core.how_to_use: The Gargantium Core is an upgrader added in the Reincarnation update. It is not a conventional furnace as it does 1x but instead serves as a utility item with a similar mechanism as the Cosmic Tower of which, upon being charged (by Gargantium Mine ores) applies the unique Gargantium Mine-Pulsar interaction to all droppers placed on the user's base. Different effects are applied based on which Pulsar was used on the Gargantium Mine that was used to charge the Core. Effects also stack with multiple Gargantium Cores. Pulsar Effects Click the expand button to see the list. ColorActivatorEffectsRedNo PulsarN/AYellow Permanent Nova Star effect, boosting Star upgraders' multipliers by 4xGreenEducation time for Ore Illuminator, Ore Indoctrinator and the Stardust Illuminator becomes instantaneous, upgrade is applied on next upgrade.BlueFire immunityPurplePoison and Radiation immunityMagentaMultiplies ore value by 1.05x 40 times over a period of 10s (~7.03x total)CyanHot PinkEducation time for Draedon's Gauntlet becomes instantaneous, upgrade is applied on next upgrade. Bonus x1.05 (~3010x). Category:Reincarnation Update Category:Large Category:Enchanted Category:Reborn-Proof Category:Superstitious Requirement Category:Upgrader Category:Fueled Category:Utility Category:Neon Category:Furnace
gargantium core.name: Gargantium Core

gargantium mine.description: A behemoth of a machine whose incredibly dense ores react strangely to pulsars.
gargantium mine.how_to_use: The Gargantium Mine is an added in the Gargantuan Update, the update name being borrowed from this largely-anticipated mine. Along with its dense, large and high value ore, it has a unique attribute where Pulsars affect the dropper itself, causing it to produce ore with different properties and status effects. When multiple pulsars are present, the mine will randomly choose one of the pulsars and its effect. When paired with the Gargantium Core, the pulsar effect is added to all other mines but not previously mined ore. More than one of these pairs (Mine and Core) can get you multiple pulsar effects. Pulsar Effects Click the expand button to see the list. ColorActivatorEffectsRed100px|center|link=No pulsarN/AYellow100px|center|link=Applies the Nova Star effect to ores.Green100px|center|link=Education time for educators becomes instantaneous, upgrade is applied on contact with next upgrader.Blue100px|center|link=Fire immunity.Gold100px|center|link=Purple100px|center|link=Poison and radioactive immunity.Magenta100px|center|link=Multiplies ore value by 1.05x 40 times over a period of 10 seconds (~7.04x total).Cyan100px|center|link=Hot Pink100px|center|link=Draedon's Gauntlet's upgrade effect is bypassed, the full upgrade is applied all at once. Gives a bonus x1.05 upgrade, totalling for a massive x3010.

goliath gumball machine.how_to_use: The Goliath Gumball Machine is an added during Part I of the Halloween 2020 event. You can have at most five Goliath Gumball Machines on your base. If you place more than five, all of the Goliath Gumball Machines will be disabled. Unlike the Swintite and Pumpkinite droppers, this dropper is not an excavator, having an automated fixed drop rate. The drop rate is approximately one ore every 0.75 seconds. In addition, the gumballs have spherical physics, meaning that they accelerate down slopes, cannot ascend ramps, and have a rather difficult time navigating through a conveyor line. Understanding Sphere Physics As the ore produced by this mine is a polar opposite compared to the common, cuboidal shape, it is important to understand how the ores work. There are no flat sides to this ore, meaning there is extremely low friction between them and the conveyors they're on, making it difficult to control on conveyors and almost impossible to travel up slopes. Unlike other ores, these ores have rotational velocity (RTV). This means the ores create a backspin, allowing them to increase their RTV and ultimately their speed. RTV works fairly strangely when paired with teleporters. Upon exiting a teleporter receiver, spherical ores with any RTV will roll in the direction opposite of the direction they entered the teleporter sender as a result of backspin. Spherical ores with high RTV have a small chance to be flung out of the receiver in the direction they entered. Instead of using ramps, Use the Ore Elevator from the shop to let your ores go higher, and use the raised candy upgraders.
goliath gumball machine.name: Goliath Gumball Machine

grand willow.how_to_use: The Grand Willow is an added in the Reincarnation Update. The Grand Willow consists of the processor, which processes ores at 45x their value, and a hexagon-shaped portal within the tree growing from it. Ores that hit the hexagon will receive a 35x multiplier, turn bright green, and get sent to a random conveyor or upgrade beam on the player's base. If successfully upgraded and then processed, this applies a 1575x multiplier to the ore. Ore sent through the portal cannot enter it again, even if the ore passes through a Resetting Device, and will instead bounce off into the processor. As the x35 upgrader on the tree does not increase the Upgrade Counter, you can shoot ore through and later process it at a cell furnace.
grand willow.name: Grand Willow

gravitational gearwork.how_to_use: The Gravitational Gearwork is a upgrader, added in the New Heights Update. It multiplies ore value by x7, but requires the user to use an Anti-Gravity Device or Ore Cannon. Alternatively, the user may stumble upon the problem of ore not being lifted by the Anti-Gravity Device. This is due to ores being too dense or heavy to be lifted up. If this occurs, you may want to consider using an Ore Collider or Cannon instead, in this case the ores will get propelled forward at high speed and thus is used in fast setups. You can also use conveyors/upgraders of the Way-Up-High or raised elevation while placing this just above it to upgrade the ore, even if the ore is tiny. If the ore is large enough, it can be placed right on top of a normal conveyor. hades fang.how_to_use: Hades' Fang is an released on Part I of the 2016 Spooky Update. Its only special effects at the moment they have is collision with other ores. Original Effects When ores from Hades' Fang makes contact with any other ore, it increases the value of that ore by 20% then absorbs that ore, taking its value, as well as adding the absorbed ore's upgrade count to its own unless the ore has a negative upgrade count. The tags, models, and effects from the consumed ore are also retained, such as fire or sparkles. It cannot absorb Silicon Excavator ores or Pulsar Octagnium Mine ores (that copied the Hades Fang's ore.) Only a single Hades' Fang ore can be present on the user's base at any time, unless the player decides to withdraw the Hades' Fang and then replace it; however, the old ore will lose its effect of being able to consume ores and their effects, and will act as a normal ore, albeit retaining its physics with other ores.
hades fang.name: Hades' Fang

hades palace.how_to_use: Hades' Palace is an Advanced Reborn-tier upgrader introduced during the Halloween Event 2019. It starts at a base multiplier of x6 for all ores with a 20 or less upgrader counter. For every upgrade counter greater than 20 that the ore has, the Hades' Palace's multiplier increases by 0.3. The multiplier caps at x30, or at an upgrade counter of 100. (For example, if an ore has 24 upgrade counters, its value would be multiplied by x7.2.)
hades palace.name: Hades' Palace

havium mine.how_to_use: The Havium Mine is a added in the Reincarnation Update. This mine's ores have the highest value in game and in addition to that, several bonus properties including immunity to poison, frostbite and radiation. The ore is also protected by 3 shields, a powerful attribute shared only by the SpookBaron-360,000. These shields protect the ore from getting destroyed by anything except educators such as the Draedon's Gauntlet. All the 3 shields can be removed by the Witch's Cauldron, an Ore Sawmill or its successor. As Havium ores are immune to poison, frostbite and radiation, they do not work with upgraders that require these attributes to work. Upon placement, it emits the sound of torches being lit. This sound is shared with Pandora's Box. This mine is based off of the now dead God of Earth in Miner's Haven lore, Atalia. hopeless bog.how_to_use: The Hopeless Bog is a Collectible-tier upgrader released in Part I of the 2016 Halloween Event. It doubles ore value, however, it has a unique effect of making ores that pass through invisible, but not destroyed. This makes it hard to tell where the ore is or if it's alive or not. This can be solved by affecting ores with a visual effects like fire, sparkles, Ore/Premium Tracker's effect, or with items that decrease transparency like the Ghastly Graveyard. It is therefore recommended to place this item near the end of a setup for convenience.

hungry spirit.how_to_use: The Hungry Spirit is an that was added in Part II of the Halloween Event 2017. It multiplies ore value based on its upgrader counter, fire presence, and transparency. The conditions to be met are listed below. Note that upgrades based on the conditions are stacked multiplicatively, not additively. x50 and x2 become x100, not x52. Variables affecting multiplierUpgrade CounterMultiplierLess than or equal to -10x50TGreater than -10, but less than or equal to -5x2.5TGreater than -5, but less than or equal to -2x500BGreater than -2, but less than or equal to 0x50BGreater than 0, but less than 20x50Greater than or equal to 20, but less than 40x100Status EffectFirex2Transparency1 (Invisible - Hopeless Bog)x5Greater than or equal to 0.8 but less than 1x3Greater than or equal to 0.5 but less than 0.8x2Greater than or equal to 0.3 but less than 0.5x1.5Greater than 0 but less than 0.3x1.10 (Opaque - most ores)x1 The maximum multiplier of the Hungry Spirit would be for an ore that has less than or equal to -10 upgrader counter, has fire, and is completely invisible. This would yield a multiplier of x500T. However, it is also worth noting that, even if ores have been upgraded a lot (>=40 upgrader counter), by putting your ores on fire and upgrading it with the Hopeless Bog, the Hungry Spirit is still able to process ores at an unusually high rate of x1000, which is better than a lot of Reborn-tier furnaces.
hungry spirit.name: Hungry Spirit

hydra blaster.how_to_use: The Hydra Blaster is an , successor to the Dragon Blaster, It was initially revealed during Berezaa's YouTube Stream on 23rd August, 2017 and added in the Resurrection Update on 19th September, 2017. It upgrades ore value by a random multiplier between x3 to x4.5 (geometric mean ~x3.7078) three times in very quick succession, for a overall multiplier of x27 to x91.1. Each upgrade has a chance to destroy and/or explode ores. It also applies fire to ores. Each upgrade has a 10% chance to destroy and explode ores, an overall 27.1% chance of at least 1 destruction with each use. Alternatively, the ore may instead only explode without being destroyed, with an equal chance of occuring. As it upgrades multiple times, the chance it destroys and explodes ores is very likely, when compared to other Blasters. The upgrader can try to destroy an ore multiple times with one use of the item, so it has a (low) chance to even destroy shielded ores after just one use. Upgrade probabilities This table shows the probability of an ore surviving after a certain amount of Hydra Blasters. The multiplier is an average between x3 and x4.5 (x3.75) per upgrade, meaning that it does not represent the maximum possible upgrade. It does not include chances of exploding ore, which Void Drive is excellent for. Hydra Blasters usesDestruction chanceAverage multiplierSurviving ores per 100127.1%52.7344≈ 72246.9%2870.9143≈ 53361.3%146649.7779≈ 38471.8%7733484.381≈ 28579.4%4.0782 x 10^8≈ 201095.8%1.6632 x 10^17≈ 4 Appearance The Hydra Blaster has three snake-like heads compared to the Dragon Blaster's singular head, and its fires converges, creating a cone shape instead of the Dragon Blaster's straight line of fire.
hydra blaster.name: Hydra Blaster

igneous forge.how_to_use: The Igneous Forge is an that was added in the The Life Saving Update. This furnace draws power from as much coal as you can possibly shove into it. The more coal the furnace processes, the higher the multiplier. Each coal increases the multiplier by x1 up to a max of x1k. The rate of decay depends on how large the multiplier is, ranging from 5 in the few hundreds to 35 around the cap of x1k. This decay occurs every second. In order to reach the max cap of the Igneous Forge, the user is required to input at least 36 coal every second, but this will take a long time. Category:Furnace Category:Advanced Reborn Category:The Life Saving Update Category:Multiplier-Changing Furnace Category:Fueled Category:Rebirth Category:Reborn-Proof Category:Superstitious Requirement Category:Medium

igneous lightning.how_to_use: The Igneous Lightning is an , a fusion between the Igneous Forge and the Arcane Lightning, added in the Reincarnation Update. It was vaguely shown on Foxy's stream. It has a massive multiplier cap of 121x, but requires a constant input of coal, as it has a decay rate of 1 charge per second. Its conveyor is also affected by this, and is fastest when fully charged. Its charge can be seen in the lightning bolt effect at the top, having a black background when at 0 charge. As it charges up, this black background around the beam disappears, becoming increasingly transparent.

The average multiplier of an Illuminator Evaluator is x2.35, assuming that the education effect destroys the ore. If the education effect doesn't destroy the ore, then the average multiplier is x5.65.

illusions of void.how_to_use: Illusions of Void is an added in the 2020 Easter Event. This upgrader was obtained by first collecting the Egg of Illusion in a variant of the Void Maze, accessible by clicking on the hidden chest in the ceiling of The Craftsman's tower. Unlike the Eternal Limbo-accessed Void Maze, this maze has an Oblivion Lurker stalking the player as they work their way though the labyrinth in search for the egg. Ore receive an upgrade between x1 and x7 and receive a protective shield. The shield serves as a one-time protection against destruction. The shield is only applied if the ore does not already have a shield effect. Shield effects will not protect ore from education effect, such as Ore Illuminator, Ore Indoctrinator, and Illuminator Evaluator. (see: Ore Properties for more info)
illusions of void.name: Illusions of Void

incendium mine.description: Can fire burn? The flaming Incendium Mine fires flaming ore that must be put out to prevent it from being destroyed. Vanishes when you rebirth.

incendium mine.how_to_use: The Incendium Mine is a Slipstream-tier mine in Miner's Haven. It was first revealed on Berezaa's twitter along with several other Reborn Fusion and Slipstream-tier items. This item was added as a winner of a challenge in the MHCI group. In contrast to to the conventional dropper that produces ore straight down from their spouts, the Incendium Mine launches ore straight forward instead. Ores also have a similar property to the Symmetrium Mine; blue fire and resistance to being lit on fire, the only difference being that the fire from Incendium ore ignites other ores upon contact, providing them with a 5 second fire-proof status effect. This ore's special attribute can be removed if they pass through items that take off status effects/fire, such as the Freon Sprayer and the Wild Spore.
incendium mine.name: Incendium Mine

incendium mine renegade.description: The is a added during the Ninth Anniversary Event.

industrial coal mine.how_to_use: The Industrial Coal Mine is a added in the Halloween Event 2019. It is obtained if the player owns the Shaving Cream game pass from Miner’s Haven 2, which costs 50 Robux. The item originated from the troll sequel game, and functions the same here: it will produce one coal ore for every coal ore used to power it. Because this item requires coal to drop coal, this item's usage is very situational and it requires some usage with Ore Replicator in order to maximize this item's usefulness. industrial ore welder.how_to_use: The Industrial Ore Welder is a , first unveiled the day it was added on Berezaa's Twitter. It was added in the April 2016 Updates. The Industrial Ore Welder requires coal to function, like the Igneous Lightning. When unpowered, it does nothing and has a slow conveyor. When powered, its conveyor speed is increased and multiplies ore value by 2x the 1st time and 1.75x beyond that, up to 3-5 times. The total multiplier of this when used many times is x6.125 - ~x18.758. Every coal ore that enters its input slot increases its uptime by 1 second.
