the final upgrader.how_to_use: The Final Upgrader is an and resetting device added in Part 2 of the 2017 Summer Update. To obtain the Final Upgrader, you must perform The Second Sacrifice (available at life s-1000+) and reach life S+10, where it will be awarded like a normal Reborn item would. Once you perform The Second Sacrifice, you will no longer be able to use The Ultimate Sacrifice. A future update will eventually bring about The Third Sacrifice. The Final Upgrader is the successor of The Ultimate Sacrifice, possessing mostly buffed multipliers. While the Ultimate Sacrifice has the ability to boost ore value with a larger multiplier if they have an upgrade count of 0 or less, the Final Upgrader instead has the ability to further upgrade ore based on a timer between the first and second upgrade, up to 1000x. This item is the spirit of the now-dead god of Earth, Atalia. the heartbreaker.how_to_use: The Heartbreaker is a added in Part II of the Valentine's 2020 Event. The Heartbreaker has a base multiplier of x2.5, which is increased to x3.5 if a flaming ore passes through the upgrader, lighting the surrounding candles. The new multiplier will permanently be applied to the upgrader until replaced. This has a total multiplier of x15.6-x97.5. With fire, it is increased to x42.9-x525.2. The Heartbreaker caps at 3 to 5 uses, which is decided at random. Whenever an ore passes through, it adds 1 or 2 machine tags to the ore, and will not upgrade the ore if it has at least 5 tags. The chances of the 3 to 5 max usage cap are as follows: 50% (8 in 16) will end up getting only 3 upgrades. 43.75% (7 in 16) will end up getting only 4 upgrades. 6.25% (1 in 16) will end up getting all 5 upgrades.
the heartbreaker.name: The Heartbreaker

the hourglass.how_to_use: The Hourglass is a that was added in the 5th Anniversary Update. It is tied to the Titan of Time, Kronos. The Hourglass has a high base multiplier of x90 which increases by +1 for every second since it has been placed down. This multiplier caps at x290, in 3 minutes and 20 seconds. This cannot be boosted by placing down more of this item. The Hourglass also features a secondary effect that increases the speed of conveyors of all items on the users base and will cap at x2 conveyor speed. Similarly, this caps after 200 seconds with one Hourglass. It can also be boosted by placing down more Hourglasses. For every Hourglass that is placed down, every second, 1/200 of the conveyor's speed (for all items) is added to itself. Simply put, assuming a specific number of Hourglasses x are placed down simultaneously, the time to reach the conveyor speed boost cap is (200 ÷ x) seconds. The conveyor speed boost retains unless the player rebirths or leaves the game. As long as The Hourglass has previously been placed, all conveyors placed and would-be placed will receive this conveyor speed bonus, even if The Hourglass has already been withdrawn. the ultimate sacrifice.how_to_use: The Ultimate Sacrifice is a long-anticipated game mechanic and obtained upon performing the titular game feature. The Ultimate Sacrifice was added on the 8th of March, 2017 and it was first announced officially on the Berezaa Games Forums. The Ultimate Sacrifice, much like its Second Sacrifice counterpart, has three different upgrades: (listed in order of priority) Gimmick Upgrade: if the ore has an upgrade counter that is less than or equal to zero, then a large upgrade of either x25 (ore value less than ) or x50 (ore value greater than or equal to ) is applied. Both of these upgrades set Upgrade Counter: 10 for the former, 20 for the latter — regardless of what the Upgrade Counter was before the upgrade, it will be this amount afterwards. Both of these are also infinitely loopable, provided you are able to reduce the Upgrade Counter to zero or less after the previous upgrade. If ores have a negative upgrade counter, this upgrade is prioritized over the other two and does not trigger the resetting upgrade — allowing The Ultimate Sacrifice to be used again as a resetting device later. Resetting Upgrade: The Ultimate Sacrifice acts as a Resetting Device, which is a type of upgrader that resets all machine tags and negates all status effects. This can be used alongside other Resetting Devices, such as the Tesla Resetter. This resetting upgrade also includes a multiplier of x2.5 and an addition of to the ore's value. Loopable Upgrade: If the ore value is less than , the Upgrade Counter above zero, and the Resetting Upgrade is already used, then the ore will get a x1.05 upgrade. This is loopable until the ore value is or higher. The Ultimate Sacrifice upgrader is destroyed in The Second Sacrifice. What it is left of it will be yours to keep in the form of the Statue of Sacrifice. This item is the spirit of the now-dead god of knowledge, Magnar.
the ultimate sacrifice.name: The Ultimate Sacrifice

toxic schrodinger evaluator.how_to_use: The Toxic Schrodinger Evaluator is a schrodinger added in Part II of the 2019 Christmas Event. Like most other gambler items, this item can affect an ore with many random effects. It is unique in that it is the only gambler item to utilize a Way-Up-High elevation, not only that, but it also does not immediately destroy the ore, it instead applies poison that will destroy the ore after 4s. This poison effect can be removed with items such as Wild Spore. List of Possible Effects EffectChance Explosion1/62.1x2.2x8x, ore is poisoned and die after 4s6x, ore is given green sparkles2/6 The average multiplier of these effects is 3.32x. Tips Though it is much more expensive than the Schrodinger Evaluator, the Toxic Schrodinger Evaluator has much better outcomes both in terms of ores surviving but also multiplier. Additionally the chance for the ores to be destroyed can be counteracted. This item should be used at the end of a setup due to its ability to destroy ore with a delay.
toxic schrodinger evaluator.name: Toxic Schrodinger Evaluator

toxic waste disposal.description: Toxic waste so dangerous that even standing near this upgrader is enough to give third degree burns.

toxic waste disposal.how_to_use: The Toxic Waste Disposal is a Reborn-tier upgrader added in the 2020 Valentine's Day Event. The Toxic Waste Disposal multiplies ore value by an random integer between 3 and 6. It also guarantees that the ore will explode after 6 seconds and be destroyed without the use of items that clear negative status effects, such as the Wild Spore or the Deadly Spore. A smaller attribute is that the Toxic Waste Disposal does not alter the ore's upgrade counter, so it can still be used in Cell Furnaces or during the education period of the Ore Illuminator and its successor. Category:Reborn-Proof Category:Rebirth Category:Reborn Category:Upgrader Category:Valentine's Event 2020 Category:0 Upgrade Counter Category:Explosive Category:Radioactive Category:Medium Category:Length 4 Upgrader
toxic waste disposal.name: Toxic Waste Disposal

transylvanian refiner.how_to_use: The Transylvanian Refiner is an upgrader released in Part I of the 2017 Halloween Update. It's a Way-Up-High upgrader with two upgrade panels, both upgrade ore by x2.5 each. Like the Quantum Clockwork, the ore can be upgraded from both of the upgrade panels. The red panel reduces ore transparency by 0.15, and the yellow panel sets ores on fire for a short 3 seconds.

In order to obtain the Tropical Breeze, the player needs to skip at least 10 lives while in Solo Play. It can only be awarded once every day. It is also minimum priority, meaning that it is only awarded if no other Ultimate-tier items are awarded on that rebirth.

tsar bomba.how_to_use: The Tsar Bomba (Russian: Царь-бо́мба) is an , successor to the Big Bertha, added in the New Heights Update. This multiplies ore value by 50x and can be used up to a total of three times for a total of x125,000. However, every upgrade after the first one will have a chance to mark the ore as radioactive, meaning that it will explode and be destroyed in four seconds. The ore can either be processed or have the radiation effect reverted with the use of an item that clears negative status effects, such as a Wild Spore. This has a maximum potential of x30,517,578,125,000,000,000,000,000 (~x30.5 septillion/3.05 x 10^25), when used 3 times with all 4 resetting devices.
tsar bomba.name: Tsar Bomba

turbine chamber.how_to_use: The Turbine Chamber is an , successor to the Cooling Chamber, both of which were added during the St. Patrick's Day 2020 event. It has identical functionality as its predecessor but with buffs to its multipliers as well as its internal variable. It multiplies the ore value of flaming ore by 21x and normal ore by 14x with the ability to extinguish flames; if the Turbine Chamber is overheated, then these multipliers are reduced to 13x and 8x respectively, and its ability to extinguish flames is disabled. While its predecessor has an internal variable of 36, the Turbine Chamber has an internal variable of 60, withstanding almost double the amount of ore input before becoming overheated. The changes to its internal variable with the use of certain ore remain the same: Status effect+/- to variableFlaming-3Non-flaming-1Frozen+3
turbine chamber.name: Turbine Chamber

turtle dignifier.how_to_use: The Turtle Dignifier is a Decimal-Rarity upgrader added in the Sixth Anniversary Event. It can be obtained by rebirthing above life 4000 with a rarity of 0.3. The item multiplies ore value by x8. If the ore has a wet status effect when upgraded by it, it receives 1 shield that lasts for the remaining duration of the wet effect. However, the shield is not given if the ore has the Dog Water effect. Unlike the Illusions of Void, it gives a shield even if the ores already has a shield.

tyrant s forge.how_to_use: Tyrant's Forge is an , successor to the Igneous Forge, added in the New Heights Update. Similar to its predecessor, it uses processed coal as a source of fuel for its multiplier. However, it can also use normal ore as well and the charge does not deplete. It has a multiplier capped at a maximum of x18k. In addition to its main effects, it also features a cylindrical radius around itself that will set ore on fire for 4 seconds. If the ore is extinguished while inside, it will not reignite unless the ore reenters the radius.
tyrant s forge.name: Tyrant's Forge

virtual enchanter.how_to_use: The Virtual Enchanter is an , successor to the Virtual Enhancer, added in the Reincarnation Update. It possesses a higher multiplier cap, as well as being slightly wider. It does not receive additional upgrades from being farther away from the mine of origin compared to its predecessor. Due to build height limitations, the maximum effective multiplier possible ranges from x30.1 to x30.7.

virtual enhancer.how_to_use: The Virtual Enhancer is an added in The Layout Update. Its upgrade is determined by the distance between the mine whose ore touch the upgrader part and the ores that touch the upgrader. If the ore and mine are very close to each other, you will get a very low multiplier. The upgrade multiplier is increased by ~.30 for each stud away the mine is from the ore when upgraded, which means it is increased by .6 per base unit, to a max of x30 (being 50 units away). However, if you remove the mine while there are still ores, the multiplier gets put to x1. Ore being upgraded by the Virtual Enhancer that is nearby will not affect any ore that upgraded from afar; In other words, it checks for the dropper the ore came from, not other droppers.
virtual enhancer.name: Virtual Enhancer

virtual gearwork.how_to_use: The Virtual Gearwork is an hydraulic-upgrader, a fusion between the Virtual Enhancer and the Gravitational Gearwork, added in the Reincarnation Update. It has a dynamic multiplier that increases with the horizontal distance between the ore to be upgraded and (the center of) its corresponding dropper, which caps at x50. However, in a typical setup, the distance usually only provides a multiplier of around x14-x22. Like the Gravitational Gearwork, it features a hydraulic anti-gravity conveyor that can be raised and lowered.
virtual gearwork.name: Virtual Gearwork

void drive.how_to_use: The Void Drive is an added in the 2019 Black Friday Update. This item's recipe is locked until the player finishes the Void Maze and obtains "The Void" badge. Once done, the recipe for it is unlocked for all save slots. It multiplies ore value by x100 and applies a unique effect, granting the ore 15 Shields that protect it from destruction. To compensate, it destroys the ore after 3 seconds, and disables the use of teleporters and duplicators. This effect cannot be removed. Using this item with the Daestrophe, in either order, destroys the ore instantly, even through shields. When placed, the sounds of ringing bells is played throughout the server. Strategy Place the Void Drive, then very fast conveyors such as Ultimate Conveyors, and place blasters along the sides, and the bottom with Mad Monstrous Melters if desperate. The fast conveyors send ores through as many blasters as possible within the 3s ore destruction window, increasing the total upgrade to ore value. Under good conditions, the line of blasters can span the entire base's length and still function. If you are deciding whether to use this or the Daestrophe for a rebirthing setup, use the Daestrophe. The Daestrophe is more convenient, less lag-inducing, and typically results in making more money than the Void Drive.
void drive.name: Void Drive

void star.how_to_use: The Void Star is an , successor to the Black Dwarf, added in the New Heights Update. This item does not only reset machine tags, but it also upgrades ore based on how much it has been upgraded. The anti-star property from the Black Dwarf still persists with this item, destroying any ore upgraded by star items; however, the 1st upgrade of the Catalyzed Star still remains an exception. The 5 second buff to star upgraders, assuming it is not used with any other star items than the Catalyzed Star, on the Nova Star also still remains an exception. When an ore is destroyed by this item's anti star property, it also kills anything in a deceptively large cube range around it, launches their body parts and plays the detonation of a Subspace Tripmine. In addition to being a resetter, the Void Star is a direct upgrade from its predecessor, upgrading ore based on its upgrade counter, similar to the Tesla Refuter.
void star.name: Void Star

vortex chamber.how_to_use: The Vortex Chamber is a Reborn-tier Way-Up-High upgrader added in the Reincarnation Update. The Vortex Chamber multiplies ore value by 5x but requires ore to be at the Way-up-high elevation in order to reach the conveyor. Because of its high speed conveyor, it is advised to use alongside upgraders that require ores to be Way-Up-High such as Big Bertha in order to reach the upgrade beam. This item has 2 animations. The inside of the chamber has swirling clouds which transition between a large amount of clouds which make the chamber opaque and vice versa.

vulcan s wrath.how_to_use: The Vulcan's Wrath is a added in the 2020 Easter Event. As a standalone upgrader, it multiplies ore value by 65x and changes ore material to neon. Every ore fed in the top adds +1 to the multiplier, +5 if the said ore is on fire. The maximum multiplier is 165x (aka 100 regular ores or 20 flaming ores fed), indicated by the volcano erupting, and ore that goes through this upgrader afterwards will be set on fire, which kills ore in 4 seconds. One should use any mines that can supply the Vulcan's Wrath a large amount of ores fast enough to erupt the Volcano, Rather than producing a sound upon placement, it instead produces the sound of a bursting volcano when erupted, heard across the server. This item is based off the God of Fire, Vulcan. wholesome skull blaster.how_to_use: The Wholesome Skull Blaster is an upgrader added in the 2019 Halloween Event. It was unwrapped from the Wholesome Chilled Present. The Wholesome Skull Blaster has 2 possible outcomes that are dependent on the flaming effect on an ore. If the ore is on fire, then the ore receives a simple 6x to their ore value and the flame is put out. However, if the ore is not on fire, it receives a more complex set of effects. First, it has its value multiplied by 4x. After a delay of 15 to 20 seconds, its value is then multiplied by an additional 3x (totaling for a 12x) but is also made slippery. A Blazing Annihilator can come in handy to negate this property.

winter snowflake.how_to_use: The Winter Snowflake is an upgrader that was added in the Part III of Christmas Update 2019. It can be unwrapped from the Delicate Precipitation Gift. The Winter Snowflake adds to the ore's value and then multiplies it by x4.75. This is done with the additional effect of removing fire if possible. However, it changes the ore's material to ice, giving it the slippery/frozen effect. While a Blazing Annihilator can come in handy to negate this effect, the ore thaws itself after 10 seconds. This only occurs if the ore is not already frozen. If the ore is already frozen upon entering this upgrader, it will not thaw out on its own and very well requires a Blazing Annihilator to remove the effect. The Winterheart has a variety of possible effects which are dependent on the value of an ore: Ores with negative ore value are automatically bumped up to . Ore worth more than but less than has their ore value squared (multiplied by itself). Ore worth anywhere between and has their ore value multiplied by x5 (which can be looped until it reaches ). Ores worth more than have their value multiplied by x3. wubium mine.description: Let's get the party started! Wubium's beats periodically activate remote items. July 2016 item.

extra.cell_compatibility: Yes, if ore upgrade counter is 0
extra.effects: Periodically plays music. Activates all remote-powered devices on your base while "Wubbing".

multipliers.raw_effects: Periodically plays music. Activates all remote-powered devices on your base while "Wubbing".

wubium mine.how_to_use: The Wubium Mine is an released on July 4th of 2016 alongside the Volcango. It has a fairly low ore value along with a randomized upgrade counter between 0 and 19. What really makes this dropper unique, however, is its incredibly fast drop rate, spewing out 30 ores in a single second. This can be incredibly useful for some situations. With its high drop rate, it also has another effect where it can activate other manual droppers present on the user's base. This function was previously broken but has been fixed since, and will work in new servers.
wubium mine.name: Wubium Mine

yuge furnace.description: A towering furnace built for the biggest mines in the world.

extra.effects: Processes ore at x10 value

multipliers.raw_effects: Processes ore at x10 value

yuge furnace.how_to_use: The Yuge Furnace is a furnace that resembles a tiny replica of the Basic Furnace. The Yuge Furnace is the smallest furnace in the game, despite the item's description and name ironically stating otherwise. It was developed in Berezaa's Twitch on November 20th, 2016. It processes ore at 10x value with no other effects.
yuge furnace.name: Yuge Furnace

yuge iron mine.description: An iron mine so large it puts the Massive Iron Mine to shame.
yuge iron mine.how_to_use: The Yuge Iron Mine is a dropper that resembles a tiny replica of the Basic Iron Mine. The Yuge Iron Mine is the smallest mine in the game, despite the description ironically stating otherwise. The Yuge Iron Mine's ore value is , landing the Yuge Iron Mine the fourth most valuable mine out of all iron mines in the game, including the Massive Iron Mine, Tiny Iron Mine, and the Iron Mine. The Yuge Iron Mine was developed on Berezaa’s Twitch on November 20th, 2016.
yuge iron mine.name: Yuge Iron Mine

yunium mine.how_to_use: The Yunium Mine is a that was added on 28th April 2016 and was first unveiled on Berezaa's Twitter. The Yunium Mine produces cell-compatible ore with relatively high value, but with the prominent downside of ore velocity randomization upon being produced. This makes it slightly more difficult to use. This dropper sees most use when paired with Cell Furnaces, not only for its cell-compatible ores with good value, but the walls of a cell furnace are typically quite high, and thus remedies the problem of ores not touching the furnace part. The following are the cannon buffs received by the Yunium Mine:

yuntonium mine.how_to_use: The Yuntonium Mine is a , a fusion between the Newtonium Mine and the Yunium Mine, added in the Reincarnation Update. The Yuntonium Mine combines the properties of its predecessors, carrying over both their buffs to radioactive and cannon upgrades. It also has an increased ore value, from the billions of either dropper to a constant . The following are the buffs from the mine:

yuttrium mine.how_to_use: The Yuttrium Mine is an , successor to the Yunium Mine, added in the New Heights Update. Compared to its non-evolved counterpart, it has a significantly faster ore production, higher ore value and a bigger size. It also has a negative Upgrade Counter value, making it so that you can use some upgraders before using a cell furnace. Much of the randomized dropping of this mine can be remedied with clever usage of a Wall Segment of any kind. The following are the buffs from cannons received by the mine:
yuttrium mine.name: Yuttrium Mine
